steps.title: Find the angle at R
steps.content: Since $QR$ is extended to $S$, angles $QRP$ and $PRS$ form a straight line. They are supplementary, so their measures add to $180^\circ$. The angle at $R$ inside triangle $PQR$ is $180^\circ-163^\circ=17^\circ$.

steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The angles in triangle $PQR$ sum to $180^\circ$. Use the two known angle measures to find the remaining angle.
$$180^\circ-132^\circ-17^\circ=31^\circ$$
The answer is B.
